title: woof quality-gates baseline record
description: Durable baseline record at .woof/quality-gates-baseline.json. Captures per-command red/green state and command identity at the time of explicit operator capture. Used by check_1_quality_gates to suppress known-red commands in baseline mode. A gate that was red at capture is reported but not blocking until its command identity changes, the baseline expires, or a new failure appears beyond the captured baseline. Freshness: wall-clock expiry is measured from captured_at using system UTC time; iteration expiry is measured from captured_iteration using the monotonic run count at .woof/wf-run-count (count of woof wf invocations). A baseline without freshness fields has no expiry (S5 behaviour preserved).

captured_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) of when the baseline was captured by the operator.

captured_iteration.description: Monotonic woof run count at capture time. Sourced from .woof/wf-run-count (incremented by woof wf on each invocation). The baseline is expired when the current run count exceeds captured_iteration + expiry_iterations. Omit to disable iteration expiry.

expiry_seconds.description: Wall-clock expiry in seconds from captured_at. The baseline is expired when UTC now exceeds captured_at + expiry_seconds. Omit to disable wall-clock expiry.

expiry_iterations.description: Run-iteration expiry: number of woof wf invocations after captured_iteration before the baseline is considered stale. Requires captured_iteration. Omit to disable iteration expiry.

gates.description: Per-gate baseline entries keyed by gate name. Only gates present here can have failures suppressed.

command.description: Exact command string at capture time. This is the command identity: if the configured command differs from this value, baseline suppression is re-armed and the failure blocks.

passed.description: Whether the gate passed (true) or failed (false) at capture time. Only a false entry (red at capture) with matching command identity suppresses blocking. A true entry (green at capture) that now fails is a new regression and always blocks.
